hard.next_step: 13th May - Met Dr. Hamidey during the summit, plans to come to Horizon Half Day workshop to be set-up soon. Dr Hamidey even visited our booth at 4th ASEAN Procurement Innovation Summit. RFP expected in 3rd Quarter
hard.ais_why: Procurement Transformation Momentum 9; AI Readiness 7; ERP Modernization Alignment 9; Supplier Ecosystem Complexity 8.5; Stakeholder Accessibility 8.5; Near-Term Opportunity Potential 7.5; Strategic Fit 8.5

north_star_verdict.headline: Early-stage APAC full-suite S2P deal on the planned path at 177 days to close: a CPO champion is developing after the 13 May summit, a Horizon workshop and a Q3 RFP are ahead, but the deal is single-threaded with no economic buyer engaged and Oracle uncharacterized.
north_star_verdict.math: Created 13 May 2026, ~54 days old, entire lifespan in Qualified. 177 days to close (30 Dec 2026). LastActivityDate 13 Jun 2026, but that is a rep-side event invitation (Clari email) awaiting reply, not buyer inbound; the last genuine two-way buyer touch is the 13 May summit booth visit. No forward slip. Pace is consistent with a 12-15 month enterprise motion aiming at a Q3 2026 RFP round; no overdue milestones, but engagement is thin and single-threaded.
evidence: Stage Qualified matches early discovery; 177 days to close is realistic for a 12-15 month cycle (created 13 May 2026).

Last genuine buyer touch was the 4th ASEAN Procurement Innovation Summit on 13 May 2026, where Dr. Hamidey Hamid visited the Zycus booth and committed to a Horizon half-day workshop (Next_Step, 13 May 2026).
The 13 Jun 2026 activity is a rep-side Clari email inviting Dr. Hamidey to a 21/22 July Singapore procurement event, still awaiting his reply, not buyer inbound.
Single verified contact role (Dr. Hamidey Hamid, Group Chief Procurement and Vendor Development Officer, Decision Maker); economic buyer in the finance/COO office is unmapped.
Oracle is the only named competitor (Competitors__c) and is uncharacterized until the Q3 RFP surfaces the shortlist.
Forecast Pipeline is defensible for a Qualified single-threaded deal with no economic buyer or business case yet.

metrics.narrative: No quantified business case exists. The Next_Step log (13 May 2026) records the summit meeting, booth visit, planned Horizon workshop, and a Q3 RFP expectation, but names no savings target, procurement spend baseline, contract-leakage estimate, or cycle-time metric. The full-suite scope (iContract, iSource, iSupplier, eProcurement, eInvoicing, Merlin Intake) signals an S2P transformation, yet no number has been articulated by the buyer. The introductory F2F on 11 May 2026 was not recorded, so no buyer quote exists. Quantifying the value case (maverick spend, invoice cost-per-transaction, sourcing cycle time) is the primary job of the Horizon workshop and a ranked move.

economic_buyer.narrative: The budget and commercial sign-off owner is not identified. Dr. Hamidey Hamid (Group Chief Procurement and Vendor Development Officer) is the only verified contact and owns the procurement process as Decision Maker (OpportunityContactRole), but on a RM/full-suite S2P purchase the economic buyer typically sits in the finance or COO office, and no such person is mapped or engaged. No MEDDPICC 2.0 economic-buyer field or call evidence names one. The evidence shows procurement authority is accessible via Dr. Hamidey; the move is to have him introduce the finance/COO budget owner before the Q3 RFP frames the commercial terms.

decision_criteria.narrative: Formal evaluation criteria are not yet documented. No use-case review, test-case session, or RFP round has occurred; the RFP is only expected in Q3 2026 (Next_Step, 13 May 2026). The AIS Strong (8/10) read and Merlin Intake in scope imply AI-native intake automation and S2P depth will matter to Radicare, but that is inferred from the product scope, not confirmed by a buyer statement. The Horizon half-day workshop is the venue to surface and shape the criteria (AI/user-experience, S2P completeness, healthcare supplier complexity) before Oracle can frame the RFP as an ERP-bundle evaluation.

decision_process.narrative: The buyer's process is emerging but not mapped. Next_Step (13 May 2026) states an RFP is expected in Q3 2026 and a Horizon half-day workshop is to be set up, so the near-term path is workshop then RFP round. The approval chain, evaluation committee, and who signs off commercially are unknown, and only one contact (Dr. Hamidey Hamid) is engaged, so the process is read from a single thread. Confirming the RFP timeline, evaluation stages, and committee membership in writing is a ranked move to influence rather than merely follow the process.

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are unknown. No MSA, Order Form, SOW, legal review, or InfoSec detail has been discussed; the deal is early Qualified and the RFP that would trigger the formal path is still a quarter away (Next_Step, 13 May 2026). For a Malaysia healthcare buyer, data-residency and InfoSec expectations (SOC 2 / ISO 27001) and any group-level legal approval will eventually gate signature, but none is visible yet. Surfacing the RFP-to-contract timeline and legal/InfoSec requirements during the workshop is what would create visibility.

identify_pain.narrative: No specific operational or financial pain has been articulated by the buyer. The full-suite scope implies dissatisfaction with fragmented or manual procurement, and Dr. Hamidey's active interest (summit meeting, booth visit, workshop request) signals a transformation agenda he owns, but no call quote or field names the pain. The introductory F2F on 11 May 2026 was not recorded. Documenting what is broken today (supplier data fragmentation, slow contract cycles, maverick spend, invoice backlog) and its cost of inaction is the workshop's core objective and the input to the value case.

champion.narrative: Dr. Hamidey Hamid (Group Chief Procurement and Vendor Development Officer) is the single verified buyer contact and is developing as a champion. He met George John at the 4th ASEAN Procurement Innovation Summit on 13 May 2026, visited the Zycus booth, expressed interest in attending Horizon, and asked for a half-day workshop (Next_Step, 13 May 2026; OpportunityContactRole Decision Maker) which is genuine, procurement-owner engagement. He is not yet a strong champion because there is no evidence of active advocacy (no introduction to the finance/COO budget owner, no early RFP share, no competitive intelligence passed to us) and no second buyer touch is logged since the summit. The workshop tests whether he brings the wider committee and opens access to power.

competition.narrative: Oracle is the sole named competitor (Competitors__c). No call, task, or Next_Step clarifies whether this means Oracle Procurement Cloud (a direct S2P peer), Oracle ERP Cloud (an incumbent ERP whose procurement modules Radicare may already own), or a brand placeholder. Threat is medium by default: a strong enterprise brand and likely the incumbent ERP if Radicare runs Oracle financials, but no pricing, shortlist, or Oracle-specific weakness is documented. The wedge is Merlin Intake as AI-native requisition automation Oracle ERP lacks, procurement-specific depth versus ERP add-on modules, and lower TCO. The Q3 RFP will reveal Oracle's real role.

deal_movement.summary: Deal created 13 May 2026 and qualified 15 May 2026 with amount refined 100k to 200k; the substantive movement is the 13 May summit meeting and booth visit that established Dr. Hamidey as an engaged contact. Since then, only a rep-side event invitation (13 Jun 2026) has gone out, still awaiting his reply. The 15 May 'Opportunity Health' tasks are system-generated nudges, not buyer engagement.

items.change: Amount refined from 100k to 200k as scope firmed to full-suite S2P
Stage moved Initial Interest to Qualified
Summit meeting and Zycus booth visit by Dr. Hamidey Hamid; Horizon workshop requested
Rep-side Clari email inviting Dr. Hamidey to the 21/22 July Singapore procurement event, awaiting reply

competitive_position.summary: Oracle is the only named competitor (Competitors__c) and remains uncharacterized: it is most likely Radicare's incumbent ERP with procurement modules, which would make it the sole-source-to-beat if it frames the Q3 RFP as an ERP-bundle play. No pricing, shortlist, or buyer sentiment is on record, so the threat is medium, not high. The single strongest current threat is therefore Oracle by default, and the Q3 2026 RFP is the event that will reveal whether it is the incumbent being displaced or a shortlist challenger.

competitors.name: Oracle
competitors.how_we_win: Position Merlin Intake as the AI-native requisition automation layer Oracle ERP lacks; emphasize procurement-specific depth (iContract CLM, iSupplier collaboration, iSource strategic sourcing) versus ERP add-on modules; set AI and S2P-completeness decision criteria at the Horizon workshop before Oracle frames the RFP as an ERP-bundle play.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Fit is inferred from the AIS Strong (8/10) profile and full-suite scope, not yet confirmed by buyer statements. The AIS parameters (procurement transformation momentum 9, ERP modernization alignment 9, supplier ecosystem complexity 8.5) point to a strong strategic fit for a full S2P platform with AI intake, but no explicit buyer criterion has been validated on a call.

Evaluation criteria and decision process undocumented

best_practice_check.summary: Early Qualified full-suite deal with a developing CPO champion; the highest-leverage levers are multi-threading to power, setting AI/S2P criteria before Oracle, and quantifying the value case at the workshop.
flags: Single-threaded on Dr. Hamidey Hamid: use his workshop interest to open access to the finance/COO budget owner before the Q3 RFP.
Oracle is the named rival and likely incumbent ERP: set AI-native and S2P-depth decision criteria at the Horizon workshop before it becomes an ERP-bundle evaluation.
No quantified business case: use the half-day workshop to baseline procurement spend, cycle time, and maverick-spend pain into a value case.

He is not yet strong because there is no evidence of active advocacy, no introduction to the budget owner, and no second buyer touch since the summit. The workshop is the test of whether he becomes a coach and opens the wider committee.
champion_strength.champion: Dr. Hamidey Hamid (Group Chief Procurement and Vendor Development Officer)

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Merlin Intake is in scope and AIS reads Strong (8/10) with AI Readiness 7, so the AI appetite is real, but the agentic/Merlin story has not yet been put in front of the buyer on a demo. Zycus is under-positioned on AI until the workshop lands the Merlin intake-to-orchestrate narrative.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS_Score 8.0, AIS_Status Strong, with procurement transformation momentum 9, ERP modernization alignment 9, and AI readiness 7 (AIS_Why__c). No call evidence yet to confirm or temper the field, but the profile and Merlin Intake in scope indicate strong AI appetite.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: A Strong-AIS full-suite S2P opportunity where a CPO champion is developing, but the deal is single-threaded with no economic buyer, no quantified business case, and Oracle uncharacterized ahead of a Q3 RFP; the win hinges on converting the Horizon workshop into a multi-threaded, criteria-setting session.

win_position.text: Radicare is a Strong-AIS (8/10) full-suite S2P opportunity with a genuinely engaged procurement owner, Dr. Hamidey Hamid, who sought Zycus out at the 4th ASEAN Procurement Summit and requested a Horizon workshop (Next_Step, 13 May 2026).
We are single-threaded on Dr. Hamidey with no finance or COO budget owner engaged, so there is no route to commercial sign-off yet (OpportunityContactRole, 06 Jul 2026).
Oracle is the only named rival and is likely Radicare's incumbent ERP; if it frames the Q3 RFP as an ERP-bundle before we set AI and S2P-depth criteria, we lose control (Competitors__c).

deal_momentum.text: Buyer-initiated interest is real: Dr. Hamidey visited the Zycus booth and asked to attend the Horizon half-day workshop (Next_Step, 13 May 2026).
No buyer inbound since the 13 May summit; the 13 Jun 2026 invitation to the July Singapore event is a rep-side touch still awaiting his reply.

The Horizon half-day workshop is still unscheduled, so the buyer has made no dated process commitment yet.
